explicit_text: My husband Paul has worked for the Florida East Coast Railway for the past 14 years as a car repairer. He started working for the railroad after studying a correspondence course to become a train mechanic. Paul has been transferred from Key West to Fort Lauderdale and then to Miami, where we currently live. The job is steady, but he rarely takes vacations because if he does, it's without pay. Despite the demands, he has steady work all year round and sometimes has to be ready to respond to accidents at any hour.
implicit_text: My husband Paul has worked for the coastal railway line in Florida for the past 14 years as a car repairer. He started working for the railroad after studying a correspondence course to become a train mechanic. Paul has been transferred from the southernmost island city to a city known for its boating canals and then to the largest city in the state, where we currently live. The job is steady, but he rarely takes vacations because if he does, it's without pay. Despite the demands, he has steady work all year round and sometimes has to be ready to respond to accidents at any hour.

explicit_text: Paul and I were both born and raised in Key West, Florida. When we first married, Paul worked for a wholesale house there before he switched to the railroad. We lived in Key West without a car and managed just fine. I loved tending to our garden there, which was quite noisy and beautiful. We hope someday to own a home there again, especially if the railroad is rebuilt and Paul can be transferred back.
implicit_text: Paul and I were both born and raised in the southernmost city of the continental United States. When we first married, Paul worked for a wholesale house there before he switched to the railroad. We lived in that island city without a car and managed just fine. I loved tending to our garden there, which was quite noisy and beautiful. We hope someday to own a home there again, especially if the coastal railway line is rebuilt and Paul can be transferred back.

explicit_text: About three years ago, Paul was transferred to the shops at Fort Lauderdale before moving on to Miami. At first, I didn't like living in Fort Lauderdale much, though we made many friends there. We rented a house near the shops to make it easier for Paul to get to work and for the children to attend school. It was a transition period for us, but we adjusted over time.
implicit_text: About three years ago, Paul was transferred to the repair shops in the city known for its extensive canal system before moving on to the largest city in the state. At first, I didn't like living in that canal city much, though we made many friends there. We rented a house near the shops to make it easier for Paul to get to work and for the children to attend school. It was a transition period for us, but we adjusted over time.

explicit_text: Currently, we live in Miami, where Paul was transferred after Fort Lauderdale. I didn't like Miami at first and found it more expensive than Key West, especially rent which is three times higher. We rented a furnished house here close to Paul's workplace and the children's school. I am getting used to life here now, though I still miss the space and garden we had in Key West. Miami has been our home for a few years as Paul continues his work with the railroad.
implicit_text: Currently, we live in the largest city in Florida, where Paul was transferred after the canal city. I didn't like this big city at first and found it more expensive than the island city, especially rent which is three times higher. We rented a furnished house here close to Paul's workplace and the children's school. I am getting used to life here now, though I still miss the space and garden we had in the southernmost island city. This big city has been our home for a few years as Paul continues his work with the railroad.

explicit_text: When Paul was transferred to Fort Lauderdale, we bought a Chevrolet because it was hard to get around without a car there. Before that, in Key West, we managed without owning a car. Paul usually takes the Chevrolet in the morning, but sometimes I keep it to visit around or go to town in the afternoons. The car has become an important part of our daily life since moving away from Key West.
